claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 15 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 15 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you please tell me how many children you would like to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm looking to claim Child Benefit for my one child. Their name is Alex and they're 15 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information about Alex. That's helpful!

Now, could you please tell me if you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you for confirming that.

Next, could you tell me about your living arrangements with Alex? Do they live with you, or do they live somewhere else?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Wonderful, thank you for confirming that.

Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

Is Alex currently in any of the following situations?
- In hospital or residential care
- In local authority care
- Working 24 hours or more per week
- Receiving benefits in their own right (such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of those apply. Alex isn't in hospital or care, doesn't work that much, and isn't receiving any benefits themselves.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

One final question: Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I'm pleased to let you know that **you appear to be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why:

**For Alex (age 15):**
- ✅ Alex is under 16 years old
- ✅ You live in the UK
- ✅ Alex lives with you, so you are considered responsible for them
- ✅ Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation
- ✅ Alex is not working 24+ hours per week
- ✅ Alex is not receiving benefits in their own right
- ✅ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You can proceed to make a claim for Child Benefit. Please note that as Alex approaches 16, you may continue to receive Child Benefit if they stay in approved education or training until they turn 20.

Also, be aware that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay a High Income Child Benefit Tax Charge.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 15 years old, which is under the age of 16. The claimant lives in the UK and Alex lives with them, meaning the claimant is responsible for the child. Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. Alex is not working 24 or more hours per week and is not receiving benefits in their own right. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 15-year-old child Alex. They are responsible for Alex as the child lives with them. There are no disqualifying circumstances such as the child being in care, hospital, working excessive hours, or receiving their own benefits. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. The claimant meets all the requirements for Child Benefit eligibility.

Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. Alex is not working 24 or more hours per week and is not receiving benefits in their own right. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.

The claimant meets all the requirements for Child Benefit eligibility.
